title: Effect_of_pressure_and_fuel-air_unmixedness_on_NOx_emissions_from_industrial_gas
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 5]
Here's the technical content extracted from the image:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   XNOx = X0 (p/p0)^alpha * exp[-Ta/TREF * (TREF/T - 1)]

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Fig. 3 (Left): Postflame NOx**
    *   Axes:
        *   Y: Postflame NOx @ 15% O2 dry (nondim)
        *   X: TREF/T
    *   Curves: 1 bar, 5 bar, 10 bar, 15 bar, 20 bar, 25 bar, 30 bar, Arrhenius fit. Ta/TREF = 32
*   **Fig. 3 (Right): Prompt NOx**
    *   Axes:
        *   Y: Prompt NOx @ 15% O2 dry (nondim)
        *   X: TREF/T
    *   Curves: 1 bar, 5 bar, 10 bar, 15 bar, 20 bar, 25 bar, 30 bar, Arrhenius fit, Ta/TREF = 10. Ta/TREF = 13
*   **Fig. 4: NOx variation with pressure at T=TREF**
    *   Axes:
        *   X: Pressure [bar]
        *   Y (Left): Prompt NOx @ T=TREF (nondim)
        *   Y (Right): Postflame NOx @ T=TREF (nondim)
    *   Curves:
        *   CHEMKIN Prompt NOx
        *   CHEMKIN Postflame NOx
        *   Description: 0.417(p/15)^-0.45, 0.0264(p/15)^0.67

**3. Tables:**

*   **Table 1: Parameters for the approximation of prompt and postflame NOx distributions given by relation (1) (p0 = 15 bar)**
    *   Headers/Data:
        *   X0: Postflame: 0.0264, Prompt: 0.42
        *   α: Postflame: 0.67, Prompt: -0.45
        *   Ta/TREF: Postflame: 32, Prompt: 10-13

**4. Diagrams:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Treact/TREF = 0.4
*   p0 = 15 bar
*   α ≈ 0.67 (for postflame NOx pressure dependence)

title: Effect_of_pressure_and_fuel-air_unmixedness_on_NOx_emissions_from_industrial_gas
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 6]
Here is the extracted technical content:

1.  Mathematical equations:
    *   S = (1 / XNOx) * (dXNOx / d(T/TREF))
    *   SPF / SPR = (d ln(XNOx,PF) / d(T/TREF)) / (d ln(XNOx,PR) / d(T/TREF)) ~ 2.5-3
    *   alpha approximately equals -0.45

2.  Graphs/charts:
    *   Fig 5 (Left)
        *   Axes:
            *   y-axis: NOx @ 15% O2, dry (non-dim)
            *   x-axis: TREF/T
        *   Curves/Data:
            *   Exper, 1 bar (triangle down)
            *   Exper, 20 bar (circle)
            *   Exper, 30 bar (square)
            *   CHEMKIN, 1 bar (solid line)
            *   CHEMKIN, 20 bar (dashed line)
            *   CHEMKIN, 30 bar (dash-dot line)

      *   Fig 5 (Right)
        *   Axes:
            *   y-axis: NOx @ 15% O2, dry (non-dim)
            *   x-axis: TREF/T
        *   Curves/Data:
            *   Exper, 1 bar (triangle down)
            *   Exper, 20 bar (circle)
            *   Exper, 30 bar (square)
            *   CHEMKIN, 1 bar (solid line)
            *   CHEMKIN, 20 bar (dashed line)
            *   CHEMKIN, 30 bar (dash-dot line)
            *   prompt
            *   postflame

3.  Tables: NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

4.  Diagrams: NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

5.  Key numbers, constants, parameters:
    *   Maximum mass flow rate: 250 g/s
    *   Maximum achievable pressure: 35 bar
    *   Residence time: ~20 ms, 18 ms
    *   Electric heaters: 30-kW
    *   Air temperature: 0-650 C
    *   Mixer length: 30-cm long

title: Effect_of_pressure_and_fuel-air_unmixedness_on_NOx_emissions_from_industrial_gas
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 8]
Here's the technical content extracted from the image:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   rho * (partial nPR / partial t) + rho * u . grad nPR = div(rho * D * grad nPR) + rho * wPR (4)
*   rho * (partial ndPR/ partial t) + rho * u . grad n(d)PR = div(rho * D * grad n(d)PR) + rho * wPR + div[rho * D gradient (n(ref)PR)] - rho * (partial n(ref)PR/ partial t) - rho * u . grad n(ref)PR (5)
*   partial n*PR/ partial t = (dn*PR/df) * (partial f / partial t); grad n*PR = (dn*PR/df) * grad f
*   div^2 n*PR = (d^2n*PR/df^2) * div^2 f + (d^2n*PR/df^2) * |grad f|^2 (6)
* nPR = n(ref)PR = n(ref)PR (f)+n(d0PR (f)
* nPRpp[f(X, 1)];

**2. Graphs / Charts:**

*   Title: CHEMKIN, 1 bar
*   Axes:
    *   x-axis: fuel mixture fraction, f
    *   y-axis: NO_x @ 15% O_2 dry (non-dim)
*   Curves:
    *   nNO(f1) - curve increasing rapidly
    *   nNO(f2) - curve increasing slower than nNO(f1)
     *   nNO(f3) - curve increasing the slowest

**3. Tables:**

NO_TABLE_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   Sketch showing prompt NOx release and downstream micromixing
    *   Components: Flamelet, f, nNO(f1), nNO(f2), nNO(f3). micromixing

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   n*PR: moles of NOx per unit of mass
*   f: fuel mixture fraction
*   u: velocity vector
*   rho: Density
*   D: Diffusion coefficient
*   n(ref)PR(f):
* X: Indicates points on the flamelet sheet

title: Effect_of_pressure_and_fuel-air_unmixedness_on_NOx_emissions_from_industrial_gas
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 9]
Here's the technical content, extracted from the provided text:

1.  **Mathematical equations:**
    *   rho * (partial f / partial t) + rho * u dot gradient f = rho * D * V^2 f (7)
    *   rho_w * n_PR^(d) = rho * D * |Vf|^2 * (d^2 n_PR^(ref) / df^2) (8)
    *   rho * (partial n^(d) _PR / partial t) + rho * u dot gradient n^(d)_PR = gradient dot (rho * D * gradient n^(d)_PR) + rho * w_PR + rho * D * |Vf|^2 * (d^2 n_PR^(ref) / df^2) (9)
    *   rho * (partial n^(d)_PR / partial t) + rho * u dot gradient n^(d)_PR = gradient dot (rho * D * gradient n^(d)_PR) + rho * w^(d)_PR (10)
    *   rho_w^(d) = Prob[c = 1] * integral from 0 to 1 of (P(xi) * d^2n*_PR / d(xi)^2) d(xi) (11)
    *   rho_w^(d) = rho*epsilon_f * c_tilde * integral from 0 to 1 of (P(xi) * d^2n*_PR / d(xi)^2) d(xi) = rho*epsilon_f * c_tilde* (d^2n*_PR / df^2)^tilde (12)
    *   epsilon_f tilde = f"^2 / tau_m
    *   tau_m approximately equals k / epsilon
    *   n*_PR^(ref) = c_tilde * integral from 0 to 1 of (n*_PR(xi) * P(xi) * d(xi)) (13)
    *   rho * (partial n_PF / partial t) + rho * u dot gradient n_PF = gradient dot (rho * D * gradient n_PF) + rho * w_PF (14)
    *   w_PF = c_tilde * integral from 0 to 1 of (w*_PF(xi) * P(xi) * d(xi))

2.  **Graphs/charts:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

3.  **Tables:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

4.  **Diagrams:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

5.  **Key numbers, constants, parameters:**
    *   D (diffusion coefficient)
    *   f (mixture fraction)
    *   n_PR (prompt NOx concentration)
    *    c (progress variable)
    *    tau_m (turbulent mixing time)
    *    k (turbulent kinetic energy
    *     epsilon (dissipation rate)
    *   n_PF (postflame NOx concentration)
    *   Prob[c = 1] (probability of finding products)
    *   P(xi) (Probability density function for the mixture fraction)
    *   The calculation domain being broken into 3 regions (reactants (where n_PR=0), heat release, products) is a method too.

title: Effect_of_pressure_and_fuel-air_unmixedness_on_NOx_emissions_from_industrial_gas
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 10]
**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   (15)  d(rho c) / dt + rho u . grad(c) = grad . (rho D grad(c)) + rho\_a Ut abs(grad(c))
*   (16) Ut = A u' Da^(1/4)
    with Da = tau_m/tau_HR and tau_HR = lambda/s_L^2
*   (17) D n_PF / Dt = u_PF / tau_PF, D f"^2 / Dt = - f"^2 / tau_m,  D n_PR^(d) / Dt = f"^2 / tau_m * d^2(n_PR) / df^2

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Left Plot:**
    *   Axes:
        *   x: TREF/T (no units specified)
        *   y: Total NOx @ 15% O2, dry (nondim)
    *   Curves:
        *   p= 1 bar, U = 5.0e-05
        *   p= 1 bar, U = 1.0e-04
        *   p= 1 bar, U = 5.0e-04
        *   p=30 bar, U = 5.0e-05
        *   p=30 bar, U = 1.0e-04
        *   p=30 bar, U = 5.0e-04

*   **Right Plot:**
        * Axes:
            *  x: Unmixedness, U (no units specified)
            *  y: TREF/T (no units specified)
       * Curves:
            * 15 bar
            * 30 bar

**3. Tables:**

NO_TABLES_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

NO_DIAGRAMS_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   A = 0.5 (proportionality constant)
*   tau\_m: characteristic time, order of 5-10 ms
*   U = f"2/f/(1-f)

title: Effect_of_pressure_and_fuel-air_unmixedness_on_NOx_emissions_from_industrial_gas
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 12]
Here's the extracted technical content from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   t/(D/Uo) = 0.014
*   t/(D/Uo) ≈ 10

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 10:** Mean axial velocity in a longitudinal cross section of the EV burner. Nonreacting conditions.
    *   **X-Axis:** Implied distance along the burner axis
    *   **Y-Axis:** Implied radius from the burner axis
    *   **Units:** Nondimensionalized
    *   **Curves:** Axial velocity contours, labeled with numerical values (0, 1, 2, 3)
    *   **Key values:** N/A

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Figure 10:** (Implied)
    *Components: Longitudinal cross-section of an EV burner.

    *Labels: Qualitative axial velocity, 0 to 3. Left: "RANS k-epsilon model"; Center: "Experimental data"; Right: "LES model."

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   FLUENT version 6.2.16 (CFD solver version)
*   D: burner diameter (used for nondimensionalization)
*   Uo: burner bulk velocity (used for nondimensionalization)
*   Combustor diameter (nondimensional): 2
*   Combustor length (nondimensional): 7
*   Mesh elements: approx. 400,000
*   Finite-volume element size (nondimensional): 0.025
*   RANS model: Realizable k-epsilon
*   LES subgrid model: Smagorinsky
*   LES time step: t/(D/U0) = 0.014
*   LES averaging time: t/(D/U0) ≈ 10
*   LES averaging time: Approx. 700 time steps
*   LES CPU time: Approx. 12 hours
*   LES model constant A: 0.9
*   RANS model constant A: 0.5

title: Effect_of_pressure_and_fuel-air_unmixedness_on_NOx_emissions_from_industrial_gas
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 13]
Here's the technical content extracted from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   FluxPR = Integral over Exit of (rho*mean(u)*tilde(w)PR dA) = Integral over V of (rho*tilde(w)^PR dV)
*   FluxPF = Integral over Exit of (rho*mean(u)*tilde(w)PF dA) = Integral over V of (rho*tilde(w)^PF dV)

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   Figure 11:
    *   **Axes:** Longitudinal cross section of the EV burner.
    *   **Curves/Contours:**
        *   Mean axial velocity (contours)
        *   Mean progress variable (isolines)
    *   **Key Values:**
        *   u_tilde = 0

**3. Tables:**
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   Figure 11 (RANS k-epsilon, Exper, LES). Components labeled within the various sub-diagrams include:
    *   u_tilde = 0
    *   exp window
    *   Contour lines shown

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   RF = Curvature radius at the axis
*   Dt = Turbulent diffusivity (inferred from the equation)
*   dc/dr = Gradient of a variable
*   T = TREF (Fully premixed conditions)
*   U0 = Burner bulk velocity
*   Pressure: 1 bar and 15 bar
*   TreF/T ratio changes from 0.1 to approx0.67
*   NOx pressure exponent: approximatey 0.1 to approx. 0.76

title: Effect_of_pressure_and_fuel-air_unmixedness_on_NOx_emissions_from_industrial_gas
text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*  NOx "prompt" and NOx "postflame" contributions change with pressure respectively with exponents ≈ -0.45 and ≈ 0.67

**2. Graphs:**

Both graphs in Fig. 12 have the following axes:

*   Y-axis: NOx @ 15% O2 dry (nondim) ; Scale (10^-1 to 10^2)
*   X-axis: Tref / T; Scale (0.9, 0.95, 1, 1.05, 1.1)

Curves in fig. 12 (left) have these series:
* 1 bar, LES, part prem
* 15 bar, LES, part prem
* 1 bar, Exper
* 15 bar, Exper

Curves in fig.12 (right) have these series:
* 1 bar, total NOx
* 15 bar, total NOx
* 1 bar, prompt NOx
* 15 bar, postflame NOx
* 1 bar, prompt NOx corr

**3. Tables:**

NO_TABLE_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

NO_DIAGRAM_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Prompt NOx correction at 1 bar accounts for approximately 10% of the total prompt NOx contribution.
*   20-bar NOx emissions
*   Equivalence ratio from ~0.1 to ~0.67
